The process was stopped by a terminal interrupt (CTRL+C).

"Note that, in Julia script started without -i (interactive) option, InterruptException is not thrown by default. Calling [Base.exit_on_sigint(false)](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/base/c/#Base.exit_on_sigint) in the script can recover the behavior of the REPL. Alternatively, a Julia script can be started with"
https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/base/base/#Core.InterruptException
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2484000932/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45055","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45055/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45055/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45055/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45055","id":1211594631,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5IN3eH","number":45055,"title":"Capture SIGINT with @async, in a script or in the REPL","user":{"login":"chunjiw","id":7267426,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjcyNjc0MjY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7267426?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/chunjiw","html_url":"https://github.com/chunjiw","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chunjiw/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chunjiw/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chunjiw/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chunjiw/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chunjiw/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chunjiw/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chunjiw/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chunjiw/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/chunjiw/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2022-04-21T22:01:05Z","updated_at":"2024-11-19T11:12:58Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Define this function in REPL:
```julia
julia> function try_interrupt()
           while true
               try
                   sleep(1)
                   println("trying...")
                   @async 1+1
               catch e
                   if typeof(e) <: InterruptException
                       println("caught Interrupt")
                       return
                   end
               end
           end
           return
       end
try_interrupt (generic function with 1 method)
```
In Julia 1.7.2, everything works:
```julia
julia> try_interrupt()
trying...
trying...
^Ccaught Interrupt

julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.7.2
Commit bf53498635 (2022-02-06 15:21 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu)
  CPU: Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-6850K CPU @ 3.60GHz
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-12.0.1 (ORCJIT, broadwell)
```
But in 1.9.0 it does not work:
```julia
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.9.0-DEV.426
Commit 4dfef57535 (2022-04-21 19:14 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-linux-gnu)
  CPU: 12 × Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-6850K CPU @ 3.60GHz
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-13.0.1 (ORCJIT, broadwell)
  Threads: 1 on 12 virtual cores

julia> try_interrupt()
trying...
trying...
^Cfatal: error thrown and no exception handler available.
InterruptException()
jl_mutex_unlock at /home/chunjiw/projects/playground/buildjulia/julia/src/julia_locks.h:129 [inlined]
ijl_task_get_next at /home/chunjiw/projects/playground/buildjulia/julia/src/partr.c:382
poptask at ./task.jl:931
wait at ./task.jl:940
task_done_hook at ./task.jl:640
jfptr_task_done_hook_61719 at /home/chunjiw/projects/playground/buildjulia/julia/usr/lib/julia/sys.so (unknown line)
jl_apply at /home/chunjiw/projects/playground/buildjulia/julia/src/julia.h:1838 [inlined]
jl_finish_task at /home/chunjiw/projects/playground/buildjulia/julia/src/task.c:254
start_task at /home/chunjiw/projects/playground/buildjulia/julia/src/task.c:942
```
In another word, issue #19467 comes back.

If I put the code in a script `file.jl`, then run it with 
- `julia -i file.jl` or 
- `julia -e 'include(popfirst!(ARGS))' file.jl`, 
then both 1.7.2 and 1.9.0 returns fatal error when attempt to interrupt with Ctrl-C: `fatal: error thrown and no exception handler available.`


Note: using `Base.exit_on_sigint(false)` does not solve this issue

For me it works on version 1.10.2:

```julia
julia> function try_interrupt()
                  while true
                      try
                          sleep(1)
                          println("trying...")
                          @async 1+1
                      catch e
                          if typeof(e) <: InterruptException
                              println("caught Interrupt")
                              return
                          end
                      end
                  end
                  return
              end
try_interrupt (generic function with 1 method)

julia> try_interrupt()
trying...
trying...
caught Interrupt

julia>

Using an function just to differentiate exceptions:
```julia
@everywhere begin
function test()
   try
       while true
           println("sleeping $(getpid())")
           sleep(5)
       end
   catch e
       println(e)
   return nothing
   end
end
end
```

On WSL(./julia -p 2):

```julia
julia> workers()
2-element Vector{Int64}:
 2
 3

julia> f = remotecall(test, 2)
Future(2, 1, 16, ReentrantLock(nothing, 0x00000000, 0x00, Base.GenericCondition{Base.Threads.SpinLock}(Base.IntrusiveLinkedList{Task}(nothing, nothing), Base.Threads.SpinLock(0)), (1      From worker 2:  sleeping 187,
 1, 0)), nothing)

julia> sleep(5)
      From worker 2:    sleeping 187

julia> interrupt(2)

julia> workers()      From worker 2:    InterruptException()
julia> workers()
2-element Vector{Int64}:
 2
 3
```


On windows the worker dies without receiving the exception (`./julia -p 2`):
```julia
julia> workers()
2-element Vector{Int64}:
 2
 3

julia> f = remotecall(test, 2)
Future(2, 1, 16, ReentrantLock(nothing, 0x00000000, 0x00, Base.GenericCondition{Base.Threads.SpinLock}(Base.IntrusiveLinkedList{Task}(nothing, nothing), Base.Threads.SpinLock(0)), (9, 0, -1)), nothing)

julia> slee      From worker 2: sleeping 42668p
julia> sleep(5)
      From worker 2:    sleeping 42668

julia> interrupt(2)

julia> workWorker 2 terminated.e
rsUnhandled Task ERROR: IOError: read: connection reset by peer (ECONNRESET)
